authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is the authoritative public baseline for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold human food and it defines execution-relevant monitoring, corrective-action, and supply-chain expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: packaging-line readiness, in-process control, corrective-action, and supplier-program boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, packing, holding, records, and corrective actions that constrain food and beverage packaging execution and closure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: packaging execution, records posture, holding, and finished-goods handling

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's guidance for industry is the official operating reference for applying preventive-controls, records, allergen-label, packaging, and supply-chain program expectations to plant workflow decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: label-control review, records review, preventive-control execution, and supplier-program handoff

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR traceability rule is the primary public regulatory source for traceability lot codes, record-linking, and retrieval expectations for covered food workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lot assignment, packaging traceability reconciliation, and hold or release review

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official FAQ set clarifies implementation timing, record-sharing posture, and practical retrieval expectations relevant to packaging-line traceability handling.
used_for: practical response posture when lot evidence is requested

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across packaging-component suppliers, plants, warehouses, and downstream food and beverage handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capture and reconciliation of item, lot, case, pallet, location, and event data

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is the recognized public standard for event capture and vocabulary used to link packaging, aggregation, palletization, and shipping events across systems of record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-system event capture and aggregation review

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when packaged inventory must stop moving through routine execution and shift into hold, recall, or formal quality-led disposition.
used_for: refusal to continue execution under recall conditions

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code for Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI publishes a widely recognized food-manufacturing code that is directly relevant to line clearance, label reconciliation, traceability, nonconforming product, and release-boundary handling in packaging operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: line-clearance handling, nonconforming product handling, and release-boundary discipline

workflow_stages.name: packaging order intake and line readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review packaging orders, approved instructions, approved artwork and label revisions, released components, code-date setup, line clearance, and startup conditions to determine whether work can begin.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: packaging-material issue, changeover, and startup verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the right label, carton, cap, film, and pallet materials are issued, the approved code-date and print setup are in place, and startup checks are complete before production advances.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: in-process packaging execution and control monitoring
workflow_stages.description: Track throughput, in-process inspections, coding checks, seal or closure checks, palletization, and packaging defects while keeping preventive-control and hold boundaries explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception handling for holds, rework, and unsupported substitutions
workflow_stages.description: Track deviations, quality holds, supplier or substitution pressure, mislabeled or miscoded product, and rework or nonconformance cases while preserving formal approval ownership.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: lot, pallet, inventory, and completion reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that packaging-material, finished-goods, lot, pallet, inventory, and supplier records reconcile across ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, document-control, and traceability systems before movement or completion is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: packaging orders, packaging instructions, routings, and line assignments
approved artwork, approved label revisions, code-date setup sheets, packaging specifications, and document-control records
packaging-component, finished-goods, lot, case, pallet, inventory, and supplier records
line-clearance, changeover, startup, coding, seal-integrity, vision-system, checkweigher, metal-detection, and in-process inspection records
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework authorizations
traceability logs, aggregation or palletization events, warehouse handoff records, and shipment-readiness dependencies
completion checklists, yield and scrap reconciliation, label reconciliation, pallet closure, and signoff artifacts
change-control documentation, packaging-material substitution requests, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: document control or artwork approval systems

allowed: classify packaging-line work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework-pending, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, packaging records, lot context, label-governance context, and systems-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final quality release, nonconformance disposition, recall handling, or shipment-release approval
engineering, packaging-specification, artwork, code-date-rule, or change-control approval
supplier approval, substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
regulatory label-content approval, safety override, legal interpretation, or policy-override decisions
refuse: start or continue packaging when required line-clearance, approved artwork, code-date, batch, lot, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark packaging work complete without reconciliation, signoff, batch, lot, label-reconciliation, or pallet-closure evidence
bypass traceability, hold, release, line-clearance, labeling, or quality controls to protect schedule or throughput

domain_failure_modes: the wrong label, code date, or packaging-component revision is used because approved artwork or setup evidence was not reconciled
line readiness is assumed without line-clearance, changeover, or approved packaging-instruction evidence
ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and traceability systems conflict on packaging-component, lot, pallet, or hold state for the same finished-goods run
packaging work is declared complete without yield and scrap reconciliation, label reconciliation, pallet closure, or required signoff evidence
quality hold, miscoding event, packaging defect, or recall trigger is treated as a local throughput problem instead of a formal release-boundary event
supplier substitutions, artwork changes, or rework paths are treated as routine scheduling decisions instead of supplier-quality, document-control, change-control, or QA escalations
the lane drifts into final quality, regulatory labeling, engineering, supplier approval, recall, or logistics release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-jurisdiction split for FDA, USDA FSIS, or TTB labeling oversight
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, the pack treats product jurisdiction as a tenant-supplied prerequisite and refuses regulatory label approval when FDA, USDA FSIS, TTB, customer, or export-program oversight would materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability Rule coverage and implementation timing
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, FDA's FAQ states it does not intend to enforce the Food Traceability Rule before July 20, 2028. Tenants still must specify whether the affected product is on the Food Traceability List or under customer traceability obligations before the lane treats those rules as controlling for a given run.

notes: Authoritative packaging-order, artwork, lot, inventory, supplier, and release records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, document-control, and traceability records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA, eCFR, GS1, and recognized food-safety baselines where applicable.

The lane stops before formal quality release, regulatory label approval, engineering change approval, supplier approval, recall authority, and logistics release authorization even when plant throughput is under pressure.
